response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, craftsmanship-oriented senior developer with strong testing culture, open-source leadership, and some low-latency systems work in DLang, but he falls well short of the core minimum qualifications for this role: professional C++ experience totals well under a year (roughly seven months at National Instruments plus a thesis project) against a required eight years, and sustained large-scale infrastructure experience is limited to under two years at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic. His recent career centers on TypeScript/Node full-stack product work rather than cloud compute platforms, and he has no formal technical-lead experience mentoring cloud or DevOps engineers.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec) using async I/O, fibers, and caching at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong engineering-practice leadership: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, plus workshops, dojos, and open-source test tooling authorship
Broad polyglot experience across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure, including Kubernetes-deployable platform he founded and maintains
concerns: C++ experience is under one year professionally, far below the required eight years
Large-scale infrastructure/distributed-systems experience (~2 years) is short of the five-year minimum, and recent roles are product-focused full-stack work
No formal technical-leadership or people-mentoring role on a cloud/compute platform team; leadership evidence is community and workshop based
